methodology: Cross-reference network analysis (49-vote connection — absolute record in dataset) + patristic commentary reading (14 evidences from Chrysostom, Lapide, Tertullian, Irenaeus, Augustine, Jerome, Gregory, Neander) + OT citation chain verification (Is 29:14, Job 5:13) + topic verification (WISDOM_OF_GOD, CROSS, STUMBLING)

evidence: commentaries (Chrysostom, Lapide, Tertullian, Irenaeus, Haydock, Ignatius — 7 entries)
commentaries (Ambrosiaster, Athanasius, Clement, Lapide, Haydock, Chrysostom, Lactantius, Methodius, Tertullian — 10 entries)
commentaries (Ambrosiaster, Augustine, Lapide, Chrysostom 2x, Tertullian — 6 entries)
commentaries (Clement, Lapide, Haydock, Gregory, Hippolytus, Irenaeus 2x, Chrysostom 3x, Tatian, Tertullian, Urban — 13 entries!)

relevance_change.reason: 17 votes to 1CO.1.18 and 13 patristic commentaries (richest single verse in this query). Explains WHY preaching appears foolish: 'the natural man does not accept the things of the Spirit.' Chrysostom's eye/light analogy is foundational.

evidence: commentaries (Arnobius, Clement, Lapide, Chrysostom 2x, Pseudo-Dionysius, Tertullian, Urban — 8 entries)

relevance_change.reason: 49 votes to PRO.3.5 (absolute record in entire cross-refs dataset). 11 votes to JOB.5.13 (direct OT citation by Paul). 8 patristic entries. The verse that closes Paul's argument: worldly wisdom = foolishness before God.

new_refs_added.justification: Already in gold_references but MISSING from relevance_judgments. 8 votes de 1CO.1.18. 7 votes para 1CO.2.14. Verso central que nomeia a dupla rejeição: 'escândalo para os judeus, loucura para os gentios.' Tópico CROSS e STUMBLING referenciam.
12 votes para ISA.29.14 (citação direta de Paulo). 5 votes de 1CO.3.19. Lapide, Agostinho, Jerônimo comentam. Ponte OT→NT explícita: 'Destruirei a sabedoria dos sábios.'
11 votes para JAS.2.5. 6 votes de 1CO.1.20. Aplicação prática da inversão: 'Deus escolheu as coisas loucas do mundo para envergonhar os sábios.' Concretiza a doutrina.

candidates_noted.justification: 12 votes de 1CO.1.19. Augustine and Jerome cite 1 Corinthians WHEN commenting on Isaiah 29:14 (reverse connection). OT foundation of the doctrine.
11 votes de 1CO.3.19. Cited directly by Paul. Gregory the Great comments extensively with biblical examples (Joseph, Saul, Jonah, crucifixion).
8 votes de 1CO.1.18. 7 votes para COL.2.3. The positive pole: Christ AS wisdom and power. Topic WISDOM_OF_GOD references.

query_particularity.description: Unlike previous queries that span multiple books, this query is concentrated in a single section (1 Corinthians 1-3). The challenge is not cross-testament linking but cluster identification + OT source expansion.
search_challenges: 1. Identify the correct cluster (1Co 1:18-3:19) from just 'loucura da pregação'
2. Expand to OT sources (Is 29:14, Job 5:13) that Paul cites explicitly
3. Capture the semantic inversion: 'loucura' (μωρία) used for BOTH human rejection AND divine strategy

double_inversion.description: The concept operates as a double inversion documented by patristic commentators
inversions: 1. Human wisdom → foolishness before God (1Co 3:19, Job 5:13)
double_inversion.lapide_contribution: Lapide shows that Paul EXPANDS Isaiah 29:14 from Pharisaic wisdom alone to ALL worldly wisdom

record_connection.note: Absolute record in the entire bible-crossrefs-dataset (1.2M links). 'Trust in the LORD with all your heart, and do not lean on your own understanding.' The structural antithesis to worldly wisdom.
